explanation: Globally, if the situation is time-critical, then the explanation must be concise. This is the most direct and accurate translation for the 'concise' aspect of the requirement.
Globally, if the situation is time-critical, then the explanation must not be detailed. This captures the 'as concise as possible' aspect by explicitly negating its opposite, 'detailed'.
Globally, if the situation is time-critical, then eventually an explanation will be concise. This allows for some delay before the explanation becomes concise.
Globally, if the situation is time-critical, then eventually an explanation will not be detailed. This allows for some delay before the explanation ceases to be detailed.
Globally, if the situation is time-critical, then in the next time step, the explanation should be concise. This specifies immediacy for the conciseness.
Globally, if the situation is time-critical, then in the next time step, the explanation should not be detailed. This specifies immediacy for the lack of detail.
Globally, if the situation is time-critical, then the explanation must be concise for at least the current and next time step. This implies a short duration of conciseness.
Globally, if the situation is time-critical, then the explanation must not be detailed for at least the current and next time step. This implies a short duration of non-detail.

Globally, whenever the situation is time-critical, the explanation must be concise until the situation is no longer time-critical. This ensures conciseness persists throughout the time-critical period.

Globally, whenever the situation is time-critical, the explanation must not be detailed until the situation is no longer time-critical. This ensures non-detail persists throughout the time-critical period.
Globally, either the situation is not time-critical, or the explanation is concise. This is a logical equivalence to 'G(timeCritical -> concise)'.
If the current situation is time-critical, then the explanation is concise. This applies only to the immediate moment rather than a global requirement.
If the current situation is time-critical, then the explanation is not detailed. This applies only to the immediate moment.
Globally, if the situation is time-critical, then the explanation must be concise for at least the current and next two time steps.
Globally, if the situation is time-critical, then the explanation must be concise for at least the current and next three time steps.
Globally, if the situation is time-critical, then the explanation must be concise for at least the current and next four time steps.
From the next state onwards, globally, if the situation is time-critical, then the explanation is concise. This shifts the global requirement to start from the next time step.
Eventually, it will become true that globally (from that point onwards), if the situation is time-critical, then the explanation is concise. This indicates a future stabilization of the requirement.
Globally, whenever time-critical, concise must hold until not time-critical, or concise always holds if not time-critical never happens. This is a weak until variant.
Globally, if an explanation is provided, then if the situation is time-critical, that explanation must be concise. This puts the provision of an explanation as the primary condition.
Globally, if an explanation is provided, then if the situation is time-critical, that explanation must not be detailed. Similar to the above, for 'not detailed'.
Globally, if the situation is time-critical, then from the next step, the explanation is concise until the situation is no longer time-critical.
Globally, if the situation is time-critical, then from the next step, the explanation is not detailed until the situation is no longer time-critical.
Globally, if time-critical, then eventually, in the next step, an explanation will be concise.
Globally, if time-critical, then in the next step, eventually an explanation will be concise.
Globally, the explanation is always concise. This is a strong and general interpretation that ignores the 'timeCritical' condition.
Globally, the explanation is never detailed. This is a strong and general interpretation that ignores the 'timeCritical' condition.
Eventually, an explanation will be concise. This is a very weak requirement that does not depend on 'timeCritical' and implies a future state.
Eventually, an explanation will not be detailed. This is a very weak requirement similar to the above.

Globally, if the situation is time-critical, then the explanation is always concise thereafter. This is a very strong property, implying conciseness for the entire future once 'timeCritical' becomes true.
Globally, if the situation is time-critical, then the explanation is always not detailed thereafter. Similar to the above, for 'not detailed'.
Globally, if an explanation is provided, then if the situation is time-critical, in the next step, it will be concise.
Globally, if an explanation is provided, then if the situation is time-critical, eventually it will be concise.
Globally, if an explanation is provided, then if the situation is time-critical, it will be concise for at least the current and next time step.
Globally, if an explanation is provided, then if the situation is time-critical, it will not be detailed for at least the current and next time step.
Globally, if time-critical, then an explanation is provided. This is a partial interpretation, focusing on the existence of an explanation.
Globally, if the situation is time-critical, then if an explanation is provided, in the next step, eventually it will be concise.

This LTL property states that globally, if the system is in a time-critical situation and an explanation is provided, then at the very next time step, that explanation must be concise. This captures the immediate demand for conciseness in explanations under time-critical conditions.

This LTL property states that globally, if the system is in a time-critical situation and an explanation is provided, then at the very next time step, that explanation must not be detailed. This captures the immediate demand for brevity in explanations under time-critical conditions.

This LTL property states that globally, if the system is in a time-critical situation, then eventually, an explanation will be provided, and that explanation will be concise. This implies that time-critical scenarios will ultimately result in concise explanations being given.

This LTL property states that globally, if the system is in a time-critical situation, then eventually, an explanation will be provided, and that explanation will not be detailed. This implies that time-critical scenarios will ultimately result in brief explanations being given.

This LTL property states that globally, if the system is in a time-critical situation and an explanation is being provided, then for the current and the next time step, that explanation must remain concise. This captures a short-term persistent requirement for conciseness.

This LTL property states that globally, if the system is in a time-critical situation and an explanation is being provided, then for the current and the next time step, that explanation must remain not detailed. This captures a short-term persistent requirement for brevity.
Globally, if the situation is time-critical, then for the current and the next time step, any explanation provided must be concise. This ensures a very short-term adherence to conciseness.
Globally, if the situation is time-critical, then for the current and the next time step, any explanation provided must not be detailed. This ensures a very short-term adherence to brevity.

This LTL property states that globally, if the system is in a time-critical situation, then from the very next time step onwards, it will always be true that any explanation provided must be concise. This implies that the requirement for concise explanations is established immediately and holds indefinitely once the situation becomes time-critical.

This LTL property states that globally, if the system is in a time-critical situation, then from the very next time step onwards, it will always be true that any explanation provided must not be detailed. This implies that the requirement for brevity in explanations is established immediately and holds indefinitely once the situation becomes time-critical.

This LTL property states that globally, if the system is in a time-critical situation, then eventually, it will reach a point in time after which, for all subsequent states, if an explanation is provided, it must be concise. This captures a strong, permanent requirement for conciseness once established in time-critical contexts.

Globally, if the system is in a time-critical situation, then eventually, it will reach a point in time after which, for all subsequent states, if an explanation is provided, it must not be detailed. This captures a strong, permanent requirement for brevity once established in time-critical contexts.

This LTL property states that eventually, it will always be true that if the situation is time-critical, then any explanation provided must be concise. This implies that the requirement for concise explanations in time-critical situations will eventually become a permanent rule for the system.
